title: aster scribneri
intro: Aster scribneri, also known as White Wood's Aster, is a perennial wildflower native to North America, characterized by its white or light purple petals and yellow centers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Aster scribneri include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and act as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
